titlestmt.titleproper: A guide to the Michael and Suzanne Kyrle Pope photograph collection AD.MC.161
titlestmt.author: Brad Bauer

publicationstmt.publisher: NYU Abu Dhabi, Archives and Special Collections

address.addressline: New York University Abu Dhabi, C-2

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-02-23 22:26:00 +0400.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: NYU Abu Dhabi, Archives and Special Collections

did.unittitle: Michael and Suzanne Kyrle Pope photograph collection
did.abstract: Photographic prints and slides, depicting the lives of Rear-Admiral Michael D. Kyrle Pope, of the British Royal Navy, his wife Suzanne, and their children, while stationed in Bahrain in the early 1960s, including their travels around the region, specifically the Trucial States and Sultanate of Oman. Also included are later images dating from the 1970s, when Michael Kyrle Pope worked for the Middle East Navigation Aid Services (MENAS), which was headquartered in Bahrain.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions governing access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for use in the Reading Room of the Archives and Special Collections department of the NYU Abu Dhabi Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged by format, in three series, as follows: "Album I" (covering the years 1962-1964), "Album II" (covering from approximately 1971-1977) and "Photographic slides," which are described in the finding aid in rough chronological order, but kept largely in the order used by the creator (with some modification) in the original metal box in which they were housed. When the descriptions of the slides contain a number in parentheses, this indicates the numbering of the slides by the creator of the images, whereas numbers in brackets indicate those added to the description by the processing archivist.

bioghist.head: Biographical note
bioghist.p: Suzanne Kyrle Pope was born as Angela Suzanne Layton on 1 February 1921 in Wolverhampton, England to Eleanor (née Langley), a homemaker and Geoffrey Layton, an officer in the British Royal Navy, at the time based in Portsmouth. Her father's naval career led to various positions, including at the Admiralty, in Malta, and eventually a promotion to Vice-Admiral and a posting in Singapore as Commander-in-Chief of the China Station in 1940, and culminating in his appointment as Commander in Chief of the Naval Barracks at Portsmouth. During many of his assignments, the three Layton children remained in England, living in Alverstoke, Epsom, and Portsmouth. However, after Suzanne finished school in 1938, she and her mother joined her father in Malta, where he had been appointed second in command of the British Mediterranean fleet.

In April 1940, she married John Parlby, a British soldier stationed in Malta, but once Italy declared war on Britain, he deployed, and she remained in Malta, working in military intelligence as a cipher officer. After several German bombing raids on Valletta, including one that destroyed the hotel in which she lived, she, along with other civilians, were evacuated to Egypt and eventually back to England. During the war, she worked in naval intelligence, based at Oxford, and gave birth to a daugher, Virginia, in 1944. She seldom saw her husband during the war, and by the time he was demobilised in 1946, they realized how far apart they had grown and decided to get divorced. While in Oxford, however, she became acquainted with a naval officer she had earlier known while living in Malta, Michael Kyrle Pope.

Michael Kyrle Pope was born in Hertfordshire, England on 1 October 1916, the son of a naval officer. After completing his schooling at Wellington College in Berkshire, he joined the Royal Navy in 1934, as a special entry cadet, and training on the H.M.S. Frobisher. In 1938 he volunteered to serve on the submarine fleet, based at times in Malta and Alexandria and patrolling the Mediterranean. While on the HM submarine Oswald in August 1940, a few weeks after Italy had entered World War II, the ship was rammed by an Italian vessel off the coast of Sicily and the crew was taken captive. He spent the remainder of the war in various prisoner-of-war camps, first in Italy and then after the Italian surrender in 1943, in Germany. He became known among other prisoners-of-war for his multiple escape attempts from these camps. Upon returning to Britain after the war, he had learned that his wife, thinking he had been killed in the war, had left him. He renewed his acquaintance with Suzanne Parlby, and the two were married in 1947.

Kyrle Pope subsequently served on the HMS Vanguard, then as a commander with the British naval mission in Washington, DC. In 1955 he was posted to West Germany as a naval intelligence co-ordinator, and after promotion to the rank of captain in 1958, was assigned as naval intelligence chief for the far east region, based in Singapore. During these years, the Kyrle Popes started a family, with their son James born in Washington in 1953, and daughter Emma in England in 1954. In 1962, he was appointed Senior Naval Officer in the Persian Gulf (SNOPG), based in Bahrain, and he and his family then moved there, living in the commander's residence in Jufair. The family returned to England in 1964, and shortly thereafter Michael Kyrle Pope was named head of the naval branch of combined intelligence services. In 1967 he was promoted to the rank of Rear Admiral, and appointed chief of staff to the Commander in Chief of the Far East, leading the family to move to Singapore once again. After his retirement from the Royal Navy in 1969, he returned to Bahrain to work as general manager of the Middle East Navigation Aids Service (MENAS), where he was responsible for the maintenance of lighthouses, radio beacons, and navigational aids for shipping in the Gulf. During this period, his work sometimes brought him to some of the islands off the coast of the United Arab Emirates, where he was an avid birdwatcher, and he also became known for his research into seabird nesting sites at Qarnein Island, off of the coast of Abu Dhabi.

He retired from his position with MENAS in 1977 and returned to England, where he worked for the Church of England at St. Albans Abbey. Michael Kyrle Pope died on 14 September 2008. Suzanne Kyrle Pope, who had written a memoir her early years and of their life together, "The Same Wife in Every Port" (Durham: The Memoir Club, 1998), remained in England until she passed away in Tiverton, Devonshire, on 23 December 2014.

Sources: Kyrle-Pope, Suzanne. "The Same Wife in Every Port" Obituaries for Michael Kyrle Pope: The Guardian, 21 October 2008; The Times, 24 September 2008; Tribulus (Emirates Natural History Group), vol. 18, 2009.

prefercite.head: Preferred citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item; Michael and Suzanne Kyrle Pope photograph collection; MC.161; Archives and Special Collections, New York University Abu Dhabi Library.

processinfo.head: Processing information
processinfo.p: The photographs in this collection are largely in the order in which they were received, with the exception of the photographic slides, which were slightly rearranged according to the events described in Suzanne Kyrle Pope's memoir. In "Album I," although the original order is retained, it should be noted the the first several photos in the album are from the November 1962 trip from Sharjah to Muscat, which is depicted in greater detail later in the same album, although otherwise the images in the albums seem to be arranged in rough chronological order. In the photographic slides, some of the dates are tentative, as for example, images that clearly matched descriptions from Suzanne Kyrle Pope's memoir, such as from the 1962 trip from Sharjah to Muscat, are labeled (on the frames of the slides) as having been created the following year. In the instances of such discrepancies, the processing archivist chose to assign dates based on the descriptions provided in the memoir. Captions written by the creator of the collection in the photo albums and on the frames housing the slides are represented in the finding aid using quotation marks, those supplied by the processing archivist are without quotation marks. The collection was processed by Brad Bauer in January 2026.

processinfo.head: Processing information
processinfo.p: Processed by Brad Bauer, December 2025 - January 2026.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Photographic prints and slides, depicting the lives of Rear-Admiral Michael D. Kyrle Pope, of the British Royal Navy, his wife Suzanne, and their children, while stationed in Bahrain in the early 1960s, including their travels around the region, specifically the Trucial States and Sultanate of Oman. Also included are later images dating from the period in the 1970s when Michael Kyrle Pope worked for the Middle East Navigation Aid Services (MENAS), based in Bahrain.

The collection consists of two photograph albums and one metal box containing photographic slides. The first album dates from the early 1960s, following the appointment of Michael Kyrle-Pope as Senior Naval Officer in the Persian Gulf (SNOPG) for the British Navy in 1962. The British naval presence in that part of the Gulf was increased as a result of Iraq's threatened invasion of Kuwait upon the latter country's independence in 1961, and several Royal Navy ships were assigned on rotation to the British base at Jufair in Bahrain, falling under Kyrle Pope's command. During this posting, the Kyrle Pope family lived in the housing assigned to the Senior Officer at Jufair. Most of the album shows social events in Bahrain during this period (1962-1963), including dhow cruises, picnics at Jebel Dukhan, and visiting guests, including social events for some of the crew of the visiting ships. However, there are also many images of local Bahrainis, including fishermen, participants at camel and horse races at the compound of the ruler of Bahrain, Sheikh Isa bin Salman al-Khalifa, Eid celebrations, and merchants in the old souq in Manama, which Suzanne enjoyed exploring. The Kyrle Popes maintained contact with the ruling family of Bahrain, and on several occasions the children of Sheikh Isa bin Salman visited the Kyrle Pope children at their home. One notable trip that was documented in this album was a journey through the Trucial States and Oman in November 1962. This trip was occasioned by a military inspection that a friend of the Kyrle Pope's, General Richard (Dick) Bryers, was scheduled to carry out, as Land Forces Inspector for British troops in the region. General and Mrs. Bryers invited the Kyrle Popes to join them on this trip, and they traveled by a convoy of Land Rovers from Sharjah to Buraimi, and then onward to Muscat, via Sohar.

The second album dates from the early to mid-1970s, a period of time when Michael Kyrle-Pope was appointed general manager of MENAS, which was based in Bahrain, shortly after he had retired from active duty in the Royal Navy. According to Suzanne Kyrle Pope's memoir, she did not join him there, and this album is smaller, with most of the images documenting a trip made on the company's main ship, the "Relume," in December 1971, traveling along the coast of the newly formed United Arab Emirates, northeast to the Strait of Hormuz and on to Muscat. Along the way, activities such as retrieving and replacing buoys and inspecting remote lighthouses are documented, and points visited include Lesser Tunb island, the Quoins, and Elphinstone inlet, at the tip of the Musandam Peninsula. An additional stop at Qarnein, in Abu Dhabi emirate, reflects Kyrle Pope's personal interest in the ornithology of the region as well, as he is depicting standing next to an osprey's nest, and later accounts from other sources document his return to that location on account of its bird population.

The last group of photographs are 184 color photographic slides contained in a metal box, which include additional images of the trip from Sharjah to Muscat in 1962, as well as trips that Suzanne Kyrle Pope made to Iran in early 1963, the Levant in September 1963 (with daughter Emma), and Sharjah, Dubai and Abu Dhabi in December 1963. Each of these trips are described in detail in Suzanne Kyrle Pope's memoir. Additional slides of the northern Emirates (Ras al Khaimah, Fujairah), Muscat, and other locations in the Gulf may have originated from a separate trip made by Michael Kyrle Pope in 1963, and while the slides are mostly dated (by hand) as 1963, some of the images of the coast and boats in Musandam and Fujairah closely resemble those in the second album, dating from the 1970s. The remaining slides document scenes in Bahrain, depicting scenes of local life, and images of the Kyrle Pope family, including the children fishing and swimming, and Michael Kyrle Pope in his naval dress uniform in front of their residence.

While almost all of the slides and photographs in the albums are captioned, additional context was provided by Suzanne Kyrle Pope's memoir, "The Same Wife in Every Port" (Durham: The Memoir Club, 1998), which describes many of the events documented by the photographs in this collection that date from the early 1960s, and which the processing archivist relied upon in arranging and describing these materials.

userestrict.head: Conditions governing use
userestrict.p: Users are free to use materials in the collection in any way that is permitted by the copyright and related rights legislation that applies to their use. Any copyrights held by Michael D. and Suzanne Kyrle Pope in these photographic images was transferred to the NYU Abu Dhabi Library at the time of the acquisition of this collection.

controlaccess.subject: Officers spouses -- Great Britain
controlaccess.geogname: Buraymī Oasis (Oman and United Arab Emirates) -- History -- 20th century

did.unittitle: Album I
"Buraimi Oasis" : "Entrance to Fort Jahili," "Old cannon outside gate of fort. M.D.K.P. and Bart with observer," "Jim Stockdale, Phyllis and Bart," "Officers' Mess, Fort Jahili"

"Fort Jahili, Buraimi" : "Officers' Mess," "Men doing early muster ant P.T., watched from our bedroom door, 7 a.m.," and unlabeled images of donkeys, local inhabitants, and barasti structures, with fort in background (3 images)

[Buraimi] : "Early morning substitute for a bath in the forts garden water tank, filled nightly from underground spring. Bart; Dick; Phyllis; M.D.K.P.," "Exotic vegetation growing by Falaj (man-made underground irrigation system)," and image of local men and boys, and of nearby landscape (4 images)
[Bahrain?] : unlabeled at seaside, depicting local women carrying bundles of twigs on their heads, men on boat in water, and two boats constructed out of reeds, onshore (3 images)

[Bahrain] : Navy House (residence of the British Senior Naval Officer, Persian Gulf), with views of the one-story building with colonnade and view of sea from the garden. Also, group of several men and women at a social gathering (5 images)
[Bahrain] : dhows, at dock and at sea, including images of crew members aboard (4 images)
[Bahrain] : images of dhows, with passengers seated on board; several men and one boy smoking a water pipe; and fruit vendors seated at dock, displaying their wares
[Bahrain] : "Manama," with images of the stern of a dhow, loaded with gargoor fish traps, prow of a dhow at sea, and a fisherman displaying his catch (3 images)
[Bahrain] : men in traditional dress, pictured with camels and falcons, and captions in pencil reading "Ruler's Beach" and "Ruler's falcons" (4 images)

[Bahrain] : various uncaptioned images, including desert landscape with (burial?) mounds, earthen-walled structure billowing smoke, with bundles of palm fronds on ground in front of it, and crowd of men with one [British?] sailor in the midst of them
[Bahrain] : "Remembrance Sunday: MDKP [Michael Kyrle Pope] arriving at church, Dick Bryers," "Tea on the verandah, Jane, ASKP [Suzanne Kyrle Pope]" (2 images)

"Journey to Muscat from Sharja [sic] in Land Rovers, with Dick and Phyllis Bryers" : "T.O.S. vehicles and drivers, Phyllis and Dick," "M.D.K.P. and Colonel Bartholomew, C.O. of the Trucial Oman Scouts," Camel train encountered between Sharja and Buraimi," "New well being dug on camel route, Sharja-Buraimi" (4 images)
"Journey to Muscat from Sharja [sic] in Land Rovers, with Dick and Phyllis Bryers" : uncaptioned images of Muscat (3 images)
"Journey to Muscat from Sharja [sic] in Land Rovers, with Dick and Phyllis Bryers" : images of doorway in Muscat (3 images)
"Journey to Muscat from Sharja [sic] in Land Rovers, with Dick and Phyllis Bryers" : Muscat bay, including image of British consulate / Embassy, and al-Jalali fort (4 images)
[Bahrain or Oman] : uncaptioned images of a traditional market, unclear whether this is from the journey to Muscat documented on the previous pages of the album (2 images)
"Manama suq" : vendors with their wares, customers, and man leading a donkey laden with goods (3 images, color)
"The Ruler's racing camels, Manama suq" : one color image of camels with their handlers, in a desert setting, and two black-and-white images narrow streets and buildings (3 images)
"HMS Jufair wardroom pool, Navy House verandah" (3 images, color)
"Picnic at the Jebel [Jabal al-Dukhan?] with Lucas's - Barraclough's etc." : adults and children, eating picnic meals, relaxing, on bare, rocky mountain (4 images)
"At Kanoo's beach-house with Helen" (3 images)
"Picnic to E. Coast - with Helen - 'Ashanti' officers and Mark Kerr" [crew members of HMS Ashanti] (3 images)
"Judy Ryan's 21st party" (2 images, color), "Navy House dining room" (1 image, color)
"Shark fishing in Algasaibi's dhow in a shamal!" : images of people seated on deck, under shade of awning, with platters of food (3 images, color)

"'Ashanti's' family day - E. and David Hepworth," with three images on deck of British naval ship, the HMS Ashanti, including one with daughter, Emma Kyrle Pope, with the captain of the ship; "E. has Shaikha Noora and Shaikha Shaikha to tea," an image of daughter, Emma Kyrle Pope, with two daughters of Sheikh Isa bin Salman al-Khalifa, the ruler of Bahrain and his wife, Sheikha Hassa (3 images, color)
[Bahrain] : unlabeled images of storefronts and minaret of mosque in souq, and men on beach with fish, and floating miniature models of dhows (4 images)
"Bahrain 1963" : two portraits, of Sheikh Isa bin Salman al Khalifa, the ruler of Bahrain; and Sheikh Shakhbut bin Sultan al Nahyan, the ruler of Abu Dhabi (2 images)
"Barbecue onboard USS Valcour, Aug. 63: Bud Thurman, Walt Schmidt, M. [Michael Kyrle Pope], Mary Schmidt, Betty Shade, Phyllis Bryers, M. Gordon-Wilson, ASKP [Suzanne], Partho, Khunji" (1 image)
"Party at Navy House for Jane Cumberlege and Melissa Pike" [two young women who were guests of the Kyrle-Popes] (3 images)

"Launch picnic with the Sutcliffs" : two images on boat, including hosts Edward and Digna Sutcliffe (retired Commander, Royal Navy, and wife), Emma Kyrle Pope, Melissa Pike, Jane Cumberlege; and a black and white image of donkeys in old Manama (3 images)
Album II
Two images of the MENAS ship, "Relume"
"Wild flowers of Bahrain" (5 images)
"Wild flowers of Bahrain" (3 images)
"Bahrain, again!" : "The Ruler's Friday races" (2 images) and "Eid scene outside the Palace, Rifa'a" (1 image)
"Eid dancing at Rifa'a" (3 images)
"Turning the drums" [Eid dancing at Palace at Rifa'a] (3 images)
"MENAS Chairman's cruise in 'Relume'": "buoy being towed to ship and hauled on board" (2 images) and "[buoy] being lowered on deck and replacement being towed to station" (2 images)
"MENAS Chairman's cruise in 'Relume'": "a dumb buoy (no light) being brought in and cleaned of guano and marine growth" (3 images)
"MENAS Chairman's cruise in 'Relume'": "fire drill onboard," "Tunb light, Pat Pattisson" and "Landing stores and local transport for light" (3 images)
"MENAS Chairman's cruise in 'Relume'": "fastest transport to the light [on donkey] and official landing place," "pre-lunch drinks on deck-M. [Michael Kyrle Pope], Peggy and Pat" (3 images)
"MENAS Chairman's cruise in 'Relume'" and other images : "osprey nests on Qarnain Island [with Michael Kyrle Pope standing next to]," "Our gateman, Ali Sulman," and unlabeled desert image (4 images)
"L.T. Relume" and "Bahrain dhow jetty," with latter group including dhows at dock, men repairing nets (4 images)
"Quoins" : two images of Didamar Island in the Strait of Hormuz, with lighthouse on top, and one view, presumably from that lighthouse, looking down at the "Relume" (3 images)
"Near the 'Hole in the Wall'" : two images of rock formations in Strait of Hormuz, and one of Didamar Island (3 images)
"Elphinstone Inlet" and "Fishing village at head of inlet" (3 images)
"A fishing dhow visits 'Relume'" : view looking down from "Relume" at dhow and its crew, at Elphinstone Inlet (3 images)
"Cowrie shell decoration of the dhow" (on prow and stern, photographed in Elphinstone Inlet), plus image of Ali Sulman (doorman at MENAS headquarters in Bahrain) and image of camel (3 images)
Unlabeled : images of desert landscape, perhaps Jabal Dukhan and surroundings, Bahrain (5 images)
"Muscat" : three images of entrance to Muscat harbor, and one image of camels in a desert landscape, unidentified location (4 images)
Image of the "Relume" (1 image)
Photographic slides
Sharjah to Muscat trip
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (1): desert landscape, of rocky plain with depression in middle
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (2): desert plain with vehicles in distance
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (3): desert plain with group of camels and their riders, vehicles in background
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (4): group of travelers partaking of meal, in shade of ghaf tree
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (5): two people, observing hole in ground
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (6): desert landscape, late afternoon
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (7): mountains, late afternoon, half in shadow
"Sharjah - Buraimi" (8): desert, mountains in background, vehicle track in foreground, late afternoon, three people standing on right
"Buraimi" (1): Al Jahili fort, interior view, looking at tower
"Buraimi" (2): Al Jahili fort, seen from elevated view point
"Buraimi" (3): Al Jahili fort, side view
"Buraimi" (4): al Jahili Fort, as seen from distance
"Buraimi" (7): falaj in Buraimi oasis (Al 'Ain)
"Buraimi" (10): travel party, standing between to Land Rovers, on desert track
"Sharjah / Buraimi / Muscat - 63" : image of sand dunes
"Sharjah - Muscat" [1] : three men, seated in field, unidentified
"Sharjah - Muscat" [2] : five boys or young men, unidentified, standing next to stream, with palm grove in background
"Sharjah - Muscat" [3] : sandy track with tire marks, and person standing in distance, unidentified location
"Sharjah - Muscat" [4] : desert, sand dunes, sandy track with tire marks and two vehicles in distance, unidentified location
"Sharjah - Muscat" [5] : mountain range with fortress on rocky outcrop at base, and Land Rover in foreground, unidentified location
"Wadi Jizzi" [Oman] (1): stream with palm grove in background
"Wadi Jizzi" [Oman] (2): group of young men or boys observing travelers in Land Rover, man on camel in distance, palm grove in background
"Wadi Jizzi" [Oman] (3): palm grove, with figures in distance, partial view of Land Rover in foreground
"Wadi Jizzi" [Oman] (4): mountainous terrain, vehicle in distance ahead
"Wadi Jizzi" [Oman] (8): small hillock with palms on left, larger grove on elevated terrain in right and center, Land Rover in between
"Sohar" (1): beached boat, on shoreline, with local people walking nearby
"Sohar" (5): group of people on camels
"Sohar" (6): group of people standing in garden
"Batinah" (1): group of fisherman and others, standing on beach
"Batinah" (2): group of men standing on beach, surrounding object on ground
"Batinah" (3): group of men standing on beach, looking down at large fish on sand
"Batinah" (5): beach, with human figures in surf
"Batinah" (6): closer view of human figures in surf, racing miniature models of dhows in water
"Muscat" (1): view of city from elevated point
"Muscat" (3): platform at sea, with row of cannon
"Muscat" (5): view of bay, with al Jalili fort
"Muscat" (7): building, made of brick, with arched doorways, two men standing in front
"Muscat" (8): view of city, as seen through archway, from elevated vantage point
"Muscat" (10): sandy path at base of mountain, with figure clad in orange, carrying basket on head
"Muscat" (13): rocky mountainous path, with six people
"Muscat" [1]: brick building with arched doorway
"Muscat" [2]: interior of gateway to city, with rifles hanging on wall
"Muscat" [3]: al Jalali fort
"Matrah" (1): view of Portuguese watch tower, with bay of Matrah in background
"Matrah" (3): the souq, including Western women walking
"Matrah" (4): the souq, with Western woman in foreground left, Omanis in background
"Matrah" (5): view of lane in souq, with hill in background and Portuguese watch tower on top
Iran
"Qanat : showing wells to underground irrigation channels" (4)
"Mirror of mosaics and chandeliers - Gulestan Palace" (5)
"Gulestan Palace, Tehran" (6)
"Dome of the mosque [Shah Abbas], Isfahan" (8)
"The Maidan of the mosque [Shah Abbas], Isfahan" (9)
"Street of the Coppersmiths [Isfahan]" (12)
"Copper workers, Isfahan" (13)
"Carpet sellers' gallery, Isfahan" (15)
"Block printing cotton textiles, Isfahan suq" (16)
"Entrance to Lotfulla Mosque [Isfahan]" (17)
"Lotfulla Mosque, Isfahan" (18)
"Mosque-i-Shah, Isfahan" (19)
"Lotfulla Mosque, Isfahan" (20)
"Shrine in Madrasseh, Isfahan" (23)
"Courtyard of the Madrasseh, Isfahan" (24)
"Panel of tiling in the madrasseh, Isfahan" (25)
"Madrasseh, Isfahan" (26)
"Taken from bus on road Tehran - Isfahan" (27)
"Bus stop salvé, Tehran - Isfahan" (28)
"Karadj Dam, Iran" (29)
"Karadj Dam and Lake, Iran" (30)
"Valley below Karadj Dam, Iran" (31)
"Gulestan [Tehran]"
"Entrance to Gulestan Palace" [Tehran]
"Road to Karadj" [1]
"Road to Karadj" [2]
"Charcoal burners, Karadj"
"Karadj Lake"
"Village beyond Karadj Lake"
"Maidan, Isfahan"
"Pavilion, Isfahan" [1]
"Pavilion, Isfahan" [2]
"Jail, Isfahan"
"Copper shop, Isfahan"
"Friday Mosque, Isfahan" (5)
"Ali Ghapu Palace" (9)
"Shah's Mosque, Isfahan" (20)
"Shah's Mosque" (28)
"Mosque-i-Shah, Isfahan" [1]
"Mosque-i-Shah, Isfahan" [2]
"Lady's Mosque, Isfahan" (56)
"Theological School" [madrasah] (57)
Levant (Jerusalem, Bethlehem, Damascus, Beirut)
"Jerusalem, Via Dolorosa" [1]
"Jerusalem, Via Dolorosa" [2]
"Jerusalem, Via Dolorosa" [3]
"Jerusalem, Mount of Olives"
"Jerusalem, from Mount of Olives"
"Jerusalem, Garden of Gethsemane"
"Jerusalem / Bethlehem, view from road"
"Bethlehem, shepherd"
"Bethlehem, peasants"
"Syria, road to Damascus"
"Damascus, old house near Saladin's tomb"
"Damascus, Saladin's Tomb"
"Damascus, mosque"
"Damascus, inside mosque"
"Damascus, bazaar" [1]
"Damascus, bazaar" [2]
"Damascus, Street called Straight"
"Damascus, St. John's head-mosque"
"Beirut, view from Higham's balcony"
"Beirut, from my bedroom in Higham's flat"
"Beirut, French Market"
"Beirut, from Arlé"
"Beirut, Emma with Al Umran's, Hampdna" [Hammana?]
"Beirut, Valley on road to Jeita Grotto"
"Beirut, Plage St. Simon"
Abu Dhabi oil, Doha, Northern Emirates (Ras al-Khaimah, Fujairah), Oman: various trips
"Doha" : image of Amiri Diwan
"Abu Dhabi oil rig" : aerial view of rig at sea (1)
"Abu Dhabi oil rig" : aerial view of rig at sea (2)
"Abu Dhabi oil rig" : view of rig in distance, from sea level
"Abu Dhabi oil rig" : aerial view of different oil platform at sea
"Abu Dhabi oil" : aerial view of oil production facility on land, possibly Das Island
"Dubai" : view of city from the creek
"Ras al Haimah 63" [sic] : photo of four individuals, possibly ruler, standing outside gateway to unidentified building in Ras al Khaimah (1)
"Ras al Khaima 63" [sic] : photo of four individuals, possibly ruler, standing outside gateway to unidentified building in Ras al Khaimah (2)
"Ras al Haima 63" [sic] : unidentified individual, standing on beach, in Ras al Khaimah
"Desert gazelle, Ras al Khaimah, Khor Khuwair"
Unidentified location : beach with hills in background, beached boat
Unidentified location : aerial view of desert landscape, with apparent structure (fort?) on top of hill
"Elphinstone Inlet, Musandam" : sea level view, showing entrance between rocks
"Elphinstone Inlet" : closer view of mountains and shoreline
"Elphinstone Inlet 63" : mountain with partial view of sea
"Fujirah 63" [sic] : view from sea of coastline, Fujairah
"Fujirah 63" [sic] : view from sea, looking toward shore, at boat, Fujairah
"Fujirah 63" [sic] : view from sea, looking at boat rowing in direction of photographer, Fujairah
"Fujirah 63" [sic] : view of boat, presumably one in previous images, moored alongside ship that photographer is located on, Fujairah
"Muscat 63" : aerial view, looking north toward old Muscat and Muscat Bay, with Matrah in background
"Muscat 63" : street scene in souq (1)

"Dubai 63" : beach on creekside, with several boats tied up
